name: WormDetection: Successful Connections to the Internet on Common Worm Ports
notes: This rule triggers when a host is connecting to many hosts on the internet on ports commonly know for worm propagation.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device
BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device with the same source IP more than 300 times
conditions_text: when the context is Local to Remote
when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:PortDefinition: Common Worm Ports

when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device with the same source IP more than 300 times, across more than 300 destination IP within 20 minutes
thresholds: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device with the same source IP more than 300 times, across more than 300 destination IP within 20 minutes
TD_list: APPLY WormDetection: Successful Connections to the Internet on Common Worm Ports on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Local to Remote
AND when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:PortDefinition: Common Worm Ports

AND when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device with the same source IP more than 300 times, across more than 300 destination IP within 20 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Possible Local Worm Detected

Event description: Reports a local host connecting to a large number of hosts (greater than 300) in 20 minutes on port common for worm propagation. This may indicate the presence of a worm on the network or a wide spread scan.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 300 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
